content:
✔ New Rule: Cross-Class Item Claims Require a Skill Check
• If a player wants an item outside their archetype, they must pass a hard skill check to claim it.

📜 Why This Works:
✔ Adds a layer of character identity and role specificity.
✔ Introduces strategy—players must consider their strengths.
✔ Prevents random hoarding of powerful artifacts.

📜 3. Post-Climax Resurrection & The “Underworld Return” Mechanic

📜 After the climax (major event), lost artifacts and powers CAN return—but at a cost.

✔ The Resurrection Window Opens After the Major Event
• Once the Chaos Event happens, a new resurrection phase unlocks.
• Certain conditions must be met for players to bring something back.

✔ Ways to Bring Back Lost Items or Powers:
1. The Underworld Bargain: Players can sacrifice XP to restore a lost object.
2. The Fated Revival: If an item was legendary, there’s a 1% chance per class it returns on its own.
3. The Scholar’s Reconstruction: If a player writes a strong literary argument explaining why an item should return, it can be revived.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 446 (chunk 446/555)
